## [18:12 - 20:28]

and see what the first few images are that are ranking for candy. Because that means that enough people have clicked on that image in relation to candy that Google thinks that is the most related image on the internet related to that word. So you can start to get ideas for what is a visual representation of a certain word or something that I'm trying to portray. So then sketch out your ideas as the marketer. This is your job. You do not just hand a landing page over to a designer and ask them to come up with ad concepts. You need to sketch out your ideas with your marketing brain working, right? And then outsource that to a designer, whether you have one on your team, you want to go a cheaper route like Upwork or a service like Design Pickle. When possible, use complementary and contrasting colors. This will definitely stop people in the newsfeed. This was a tip from my designer. And also choose your fonts wisely. I see a lot of people using overlaying text on images, which is totally fine. As long as you keep everything below 20%, same thing with a video, the thumbnail just needs to be below 20% text.
